detailedExemplarData.title: How can BÃ¨zier curves be utilised to model paths and movement in video games?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and focused topic communication regarding BÃ©zier curves and their application in video game movement
Explicitly stated, focused research question tightly linked to all sections of the study
weaknesses: Minor phrasing lapses and typographical errors that slightly reduce scholarly tone
Methodology rationale could be more critically justified (e.g., choice of quadratic example)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation consistently communicates its topic and purpose with precision, and the research question is clear, focused, and effectively drives the analysis. The methodology is broadly appropriate and reproducible but lacks critical discussion of source selection and more advanced experimental design. This aligns with the 5/6 band.

strengths: Solid command of mathematical concepts and accurate derivations (e.g., De Casteljau algorithm, Bernstein polynomials)
Consistent and accurate use of subject-specific terminology and programming concepts
weaknesses: Occasional algebraic and spelling inconsistencies (e.g., âBÃ¨zierâ vs âBÃ©zierâ, âDe Castijauâ)
Missing deeper treatment of numerical stability and more advanced theoretical implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates excellent subject knowledge applied correctly and uses terminology accurately. Minor typographical and completeness issues prevent a fullâmarks award, placing it securely in the 5/6 band.

strengths: Good integration of a variety of relevant sources with clear links to the research question
Logical, step-by-step analysis (mathematical derivations and Unity case study) supporting coherent conclusions
weaknesses: Lacks quantitative data (e.g., performance benchmarks) to rigorously support claims about efficiency
Evaluation remains somewhat general, with limited critical weighting of alternative techniques
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student conducts solid research and provides clear analysis and coherent argumentation, but the absence of empirical benchmarking and more critical evaluation of alternatives limits the overall depth. These characteristics align with a mid-range performance on the 0â12 scale, meriting 6 marks.

strengths: Clear, logical structure with wellânumbered sections, figures, and table of contents
Effective use of figures that enhance conceptual understanding
weaknesses: Inconsistent page numbering and citation formatting issues (superscript breaks, erratic footnotes)
Section numbering gaps and occasional informal tone detract from overall polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is generally clear and supports reader navigation, but formatting inconsistencies and uneven academic tone prevent top marks. This performance corresponds to the 3/4 band at the lower half.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research questionâ"How can BÃ©zier curves be utilised to model paths and movement in video games?"âis stated prominently, is focused, and is tightly linked to the mathematical and coding discussion that follows.  All sections (mathematical derivation, Unity coding, evaluation) are clearly aligned with answering this question, showing effective connection.  The question could be narrowed further (e.g. specific game genre or performance metric), so a mark of 5 rather than 6 is appropriate.

The mathematical derivations and Unity case-study provide clear, logically sequenced analysis, and conclusions about local control and efficiency are generally supported by explanations.  However, quantitative data (e.g. frame-time comparisons) are absent, and some assertions about computational load remain qualitative.  The depth of analysis is therefore solid but not consistently rigorous, warranting a mid-band mark of 4.

Mathematical terminologyâcontrol points, parameter t, Bernstein polynomial, local control, affine transformationsâis used accurately and consistently throughout.  Programming terms such as Gizmos, Transform, coroutine are also applied correctly.  Minor spelling inconsistencies (e.g. "BÃ¨zier" vs "BÃ©zier") are present but do not hinder clarity, so the work fits securely in the highest band at the lower mark of 5.

The student supplies a title page, table of contents, word count and labelled figures.  However, page numbering is not clearly evidenced in the submission extract, and footnote formatting is sometimes erratic.  Citation style varies and several web addresses break across lines.  The basic layout considerations are met but not faultless, corresponding to the 1-2 band with the upper mark of 2.

The student demonstrates solid command of relevant mathematics: linear interpolation, De Casteljau algorithm, Bernstein polynomials, and cubic curve equations are derived correctly and applied in code.  The Unity implementation shows understanding of how mathematical expressions translate to programming constructs.  Occasional algebraic typos and the absence of deeper treatment of numerical stability prevent the work from being flawless, so a 5 is awarded rather than the full 6.

The student presents a clearly-defined topicâusing BÃ©zier curves to model paths and movement in video gamesâand states this purpose explicitly in both the title block and the opening paragraph.  Throughout the essay the focus on BÃ©zier construction, software generation and Unity implementation is maintained without digression, demonstrating accurate and effective communication of purpose.  Minor lapses in phrasing and occasional typographical errors do not materially impede comprehension, so the work sits solidly in the top band, albeit nearer the lower end because the phrasing could be more concise and scholarly.

The essay follows a logical EE structure with abstracted contents, numbered sections, figures, appendix and bibliography, all of which aid understanding.  Headings align well with the research trajectory.  Some section numbering is inconsistent (e.g., 4.4 then 4.6), but overall the structure supports clarity, meriting the higher band for Presentation.

The bibliography and in-text citations show a reasonable variety of sources that are broadly relevant.  The student integrates these sources into the narrative (history, applications, algorithms) with clear links to the research question.  Some key references are secondary or tutorial rather than peer-reviewed, and there is limited evidence of source triangulation, placing the work in the middle band but toward its upper boundary (5/6).

The student offers a reasoned discussion of benefits, efficiency, and limitations, referencing the literature and personal coding experience.  Evaluation is present but remains somewhat general, lacking critical weighting of evidence or exploration of alternative modelling techniques.  The argument is coherent yet only partially critical, fitting the 4-6 band at the lower end (4).

The methodology combines theoretical derivation, software experimentation, and comparison to polynomial alternatives.  A range of academic, web and video sources is cited, and the Unity case-study is described in enough detail for replication.  Nonetheless, many sources are popular or tutorial-style, and the criteria for selection are not critically discussed; primary data or quantitative benchmarking of performance is absent.  Thus the methodology is "mostly complete" rather than exemplary, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: Inclusion of a word count demonstrates adherence to formatting requirements and supports transparency of the report length.
The table of contents structure is clear and supports easy navigation, with all major sections and page references listed.
Figure 1 effectively illustrates how parameter $$t$$ maps to a point on the line, reinforcing conceptual understanding.
Figure 5 caption uses inconsistent notation â$$P_{0,2}$$â instead of the defined â$$P_{0...2}$$â; align notation for precision.
The superscript formatting of citation 11 is irregular and breaks across lines; apply consistent citation style formatting throughout.
The split of the research question across two lines interrupts readability; consolidate the full question into a single block for clarity.
The spelling of âBÃ©zierâ appears inconsistently (âBÃ¨zierâ); standardize this terminology throughout to uphold academic precision.
Section numbering in the contents jumps from 4.4 to 4.6 without 4.5, which may confuse readers; revise numbering for completeness.
Page numbering is not visible in the footer here; ensure consistent placement of page numbers throughout the document.
The introduction demonstrates clear personal engagement and contextualizes why BÃ©zier curves are relevant to the investigation.
The language in the opening paragraphs is overly informal and narrative; adopt a more concise academic tone to strengthen scholarly voice.
The sentence fragment âIn investigating this process. I encountered BÃ¨zier curvesâ¦â disrupts flow; merge clauses for grammatical clarity.
Footnote superscripts on BÃ©zier definition are inconsistent (e.g. â45â); ensure each source uses a distinct, correctly ordered marker.
The overview of BÃ©zier curve properties is well articulated, showing strong command of subject-specific terminology and context.
The term âDe Castijauâs algorithmâ is misspelled; correct to âDe Casteljauâs algorithmâ to maintain mathematical precision.
After presenting $$y - y_1 = m(x - x_1)$$, explicitly derive the parametric form $$x(t)=x_0(1-t)+x_1t$$ to bridge to BÃ©zier interpolation.
The word âinterplantâ is a typo; revise to âinterpolantâ to avoid confusion in technical descriptions.
The parametric derivation $$x(t)=x_0+(x_1-x_0)t$$ is correct and demonstrates solid understanding of linear interpolation.
When introducing Bernstein polynomials, clarify that these are degree-1 cases and include the general $$B_{i,n}(t)$$ form for completeness.
The transition into section 2.2 lacks a rationale for selecting the quadratic example; briefly explain why this degree was chosen.
The computation of $$y_{1...2}$$ is missing the final numerical step; show both the interpolation and resulting coordinate explicitly.
Step-by-step calculation for intermediate points (e.g., $$P_{0...1}$$ and $$P_{1...2}$$) is well structured and supports comprehension of the algorithm.
When presenting the final interpolation equation for $$P_{0...2}$$, annotate each arithmetic step to improve transparency for readers.
Recursive linear interpolation is accurately described, linking conceptual steps to the De Casteljau process effectively.
The YouTube citation lacks a timestamp and author detail; include both to meet academic referencing standards.
